Wiki Instructions
Fund OpenEMR Certification Together
Meeting these requirements ensures thousands of practices stay compliant, interoperable, and free from vendor lock-in. We need $200,000 to fund the development work, testing, and certification that keeps OpenEMR certified for everyone. Learn more about
OpenEMR's ONC Certification
and
donate to the campaign
!
The OpenEMR Documentation Wiki
OpenEMR
is a Free and Open Source electronic health records and medical practice management application. It features fully integrated electronic health records, practice management, scheduling, electronic billing, internationalization, free support, a vibrant community, and
a whole lot more
. It can run on Windows, Linux, Mac OS X, and many other platforms.

.

Transparency and Disclosure Requirements
This Health IT Module is compliant with the ONC Certification Criteria for Health IT and has been certified by an ONC-ACB in accordance with the applicable certification criteria adopted by the Secretary of Health and Human Services. This certification does not represent an endorsement by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.
Additional Costs
OpenEMR is
free software
. However, one third-party service is required to fulfill ONC certification compliance: the
EMR Direct phimail service
(approximate pricing:
$300 setup fee
+
$150/year per provider
).
Retired
OpenEMR 7.0 ONC Ambulatory EHR Certification
(retired 2/27/2026)
: This certification was retired on February 27, 2026. Users needed to upgrade to
OpenEMR 8.0.0
to maintain ONC certification. See this page if interested in how certification was achieved
7.0 Certification Project Page

SVAP
Standards Version Advancement Process (SVAP) Advance Notice
Notice Date:
January 22, 2026
Implementation Date:
December 24, 2025 (OpenEMR 7.0.4)
(A) Intent to Update
The OpenEMR Foundation is updating OpenEMR certified Health IT Module to the following ONC-approved advanced versions of standards under the Standards Version Advancement Process (SVAP):
Standard/Implementation Guide
Previous Version
Updated Version
Certification Criteria
HL7 FHIR US Core Implementation Guide
STU 3.1.1
STU 8.0.0
§ 170.315(g)(10)
HL7 FHIR SMART Application Launch Framework Implementation Guide
Release 1.0.0
Release 2.2.0
§ 170.315(g)(10)
HL7 FHIR Bulk Data Access (Flat FHIR)
v1.0.0: STU 1
v2.0.0: STU 2
§ 170.315(g)(10)
USCDI
Version 1
Version 5
§ 170.315(g)(10), § 170.315(b)(11)
(B) Real World Interoperability Impact
These updates maintain backward compatibility with existing systems. FHIR APIs remain accessible to existing third-party applications. No disruption is expected to current data exchange. Existing systems can be configured to fall back to earlier US Core standards of 3.1.1 or 7.0.0 to support their existing software ecosystems while those systems upgrade to support US Core 8.0.0. This is done through the Config -> Connectors -> Maximum supported version for US Core FHIR Implementation Guide setting.
The real world interoperability of this impact is that existing systems will be able to connect with additional capabilities of healthcare systems that exchange the data elements contained in USCDI V5 and US Core 8.0.0 including such areas as insurance coverage, SDOH information, advance care directives, additional clinical note representations, and many other additions.
(C) Version Support Policy
OpenEMR encourages upgrading to the latest release via
OpenEMR Downloads
and questions can be directed to
the OpenEMR Community Forum
.
US Core 6.1.0 and USCDI V3 will not be supported as part of the (g)(10) FHIR interoperability exchange. Users needing to interoperate exclusively with USCDI V3 elements will need to utilize the (g)(6)-(g)(9) or (b)(1) CCCDA communication system for their interoperability exchange needs if USCDI V3 only support is needed.
OpenEMR's current development roadmap is to support the deprecated USCDI V1 and US Core 3.1.1 interoperability requirements for a period of 1 year after which our current plans is to deprecate that functionality.
